title: Increase in anthropogenic inputs of nitrogen and phosphorus
y_axis_title: Anthropogenic N and P inputs to the biosphere
subtitle: The visualisation shows the development of anthropogenic nitrogen (N) and phosphorus (P) inputs to the biosphere (mean, Tg per year) since the industrial revolution (1860).
source: Peñuelas, J., Poulter, B., Sardans, J., Ciais, P., Van der Velde, M., Bopp, L., ... & Janssens, I. A. (2013). Human-induced nitrogen–phosphorus imbalances alter natural and managed ecosystems across the globe. Nature communications, 4(1), 2934. https://doi.org/10.1038/ncomms3934
visdescription: The visualisation shows the development of anthropogenic nitrogen (N) and phosphorus (P) inputs to the biosphere (mean, Tg per year) since the industrial revolution (1860).

limitations.projection: The 2050 values in the 2013 source are projections, not observations. The renderer separates the 2010–2050 segment with a dashed line and hollow markers.
